text.title: Microservices Inter-Service Communication Examples
text.summary: Worked example: choose style for OrderCreated → (billing, shipping, analytics).

example.context: order-service creates an order; billing must charge the card, shipping must schedule pickup, analytics must update dashboards. Latency budget 1s for OrderCreated response (post-create webhook OK); fan_out_consumers = 3 and growing.
example.walk: Step 1: attributes — caller=order-service, downstreams=[billing,shipping,analytics], fan_out=3+, durability=must persist (lost OrderCreated = lost charge), QPS=200, schema evolves quarterly. Step 2: decision tree — durability_required + fan_out>1 → async messaging. Pick Kafka (higher throughput, log-replay). Step 3: order-created.proto registered in schema registry (https://schema-registry.example.com/order-created/v1). Step 4: idempotency_key field = order_id; consumers dedupe via Redis. Step 5: ADR-042 written: 'OrderCreated published to Kafka topic orders.created.v1; consumers billing/shipping/analytics subscribe independently. Producer doesn't know consumers. Schema evolves via additive-only backward-compat rule.'
example.output: Producer publishes one event; consumers added/removed without producer changes; idempotent on consumer redelivery.
